title: Sesi Language Specification (v1.3)
description: A concise, legible, and practical programming language designed for frictionless script development, process orchestration, and integration with external models.
summary: Sesi is a minimal, buildable language featuring a clean syntax and a robust module system. It emphasizes transparency and practicality with built-in support for process spawning, file system operations, and cross-platform library management.

code_examples.code:
let x = 10
let y = 20
print x + y
fn add(a: number, b: number) {print a + b}

key_rules: Execution is strictly blocking; concurrency is handled via spawn().
Variables are declared using 'let'; functions use 'fn'.
Module resolution follows a strict priority: Script directory -> Working directory -> SESI_PATH -> ~/.sesi/lib.
Structured output requires a schema definition and provides type-safe access to returned fields.
Null propagation: Operations on null return null without throwing exceptions.
Lexical scoping applies; inner scopes shadow outer scopes.
